mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity, which drives higher corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return is positive in periods where FEDFUNDS > 5% over any 3-year window (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Low industrial production combined with high inflation indicates a supply-constrained stagflationary environment that erodes corporate margins and depresses equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of INDPRO below 100 and CPIAUCSL above 300 does not lead to negative 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase, rising housing starts reflect robust demand and positive economic momentum, driving higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods with UNRATE < 4% and GDPC1 growth > 2%, rising HOUST (above its 3-month average) is associated with negative 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales signals strong consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If weekly forward returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS growth over any 1-year rolling window (correlation < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4%, it signals labor market slack and declining household income, which reduces consumer spending and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return is positive when UNRATE > 4% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rise in GDP and unemployment characterizes a late-cycle slowdown where economic growth peaks while labor market weakens, foreshadowing declines in equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint condition of positive GDPC1 growth and rising UNRATE (over 6 months) does not predict negative 60-day returns (F1 score < 0.5).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, accelerating nonfarm payroll growth signals strengthening labor demand and income, which supports consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods where UNRATE is dropping from a recent peak, rising PAYEMS does not lead to above-median 20-day forward returns (Sharpe ratio < 0.5).
